event_text: While batting a shiny pebble around, m_c misjudged a pounce and smashed {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} tiny little snout into it.

m_c isn't as young as {PRONOUN/m_c/subject} used to be, and {PRONOUN/m_c/subject} {VERB/m_c/decline/declines} giving another badger ride to nap off the happy soreness that comes with being the kits' current favorite chew toy.

log.cats_from: m_c gave r_c the best badger rides as a kit.

event_text: m_c claims {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} bruises are from battle training. m_c is lying; they're actually from a misjudged pounce.
m_c plays with a kit after training instead of resting. The lack of downtime leaves {PRONOUN/m_c/object} sore and aching.
m_c walked into a boulder. Ow.
m_c was running around on patrol when {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} paw landed right in a rabbit hole. {PRONOUN/m_c/subject/CAP} limped the whole way home.
m_c got hurt while chasing fireflies through camp.
m_c and r_c return to camp, worn but triumphant after their victory against o_c_n.

event_text: After a victorious battle against a o_c_n cat, m_c returns to camp with pride… only to trip over at the entrance, spraining {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} paw.
m_c hides in a cramped nook, planning to ambush the next o_c_n patrol. When no one shows, {PRONOUN/m_c/subject} {VERB/m_c/return/returns} to camp aching and stiff.
m_c is punished by r_c for holding back against a o_c_n cat.
